description: Agent architectures, tool use, multi-agent coordination, memory, planning, RAG, and function calling patterns
source: ReAct (Yao et al. 2023), Toolformer, BDI architecture, LangChain/LlamaIndex patterns, multi-agent systems literature

triples.subject: AI agent
triples.object: system that perceives environment and takes actions to achieve goals

triples.subject: LLM agent
triples.object: language model with tool use, memory, and planning

triples.subject: ReAct
triples.object: pure reasoning or pure acting on knowledge tasks

triples.subject: tool use
triples.object: interact with external systems and APIs

triples.subject: tool use
triples.object: LLM inability to perform precise computation

triples.subject: function calling
triples.object: tool use via structured JSON output

triples.subject: function calling
triples.object: function schema definition with parameter types

triples.subject: function calling
triples.object: type-safe interaction between LLM and external code

triples.subject: Toolformer
triples.object: LLM to decide when and how to call tools via self-supervision

triples.subject: beliefs
triples.object: agent's current model of the world

triples.subject: desires
triples.object: states the agent wants to achieve

triples.subject: intentions
triples.object: plans the agent has committed to execute

triples.subject: reflexion
triples.object: verbal self-reflection to improve on failed attempts

triples.subject: reflexion
triples.object: failure analysis in episodic memory for future attempts

triples.subject: RAG
triples.object: retrieval from knowledge store with LLM generation

triples.subject: RAG
triples.object: hallucination by grounding generation in retrieved facts

triples.subject: vector database
triples.object: approximate nearest neighbor search over embeddings

triples.subject: context window
triples.object: amount of information agent can process at once

triples.subject: episodic memory
triples.object: learning from past successes and failures

triples.subject: multi-agent systems
triples.object: multiple specialized agents collaborating on tasks

triples.subject: orchestrator-worker pattern
triples.object: central agent that delegates to specialist agents

triples.subject: peer-to-peer pattern
triples.object: agents negotiating directly without central control

triples.subject: blackboard architecture
triples.object: shared workspace that agents read from and write to

triples.subject: agent specialization
triples.object: quality by limiting each agent's scope

triples.subject: agent handoff
triples.object: conversation or task context between specialized agents

triples.subject: self-ask
triples.object: complex question into simpler follow-up questions

triples.subject: code interpreter
triples.object: data analysis, computation, and file manipulation

triples.subject: sandbox execution
triples.object: agent code from causing system damage

triples.subject: grounding
triples.object: agent's language understanding to real-world state

triples.subject: agent evaluation
triples.object: task completion rate, efficiency, and safety

triples.subject: MCP
triples.object: how LLMs connect to external data sources and tools

triples.subject: agentic loop failure modes
triples.object: infinite loops, hallucinated tool calls, context overflow

triples.subject: retry with backoff
triples.object: transient tool failures in agent loops

triples.subject: observation truncation
triples.object: tool output from consuming entire context window

triples.subject: system prompt
triples.object: agent's role, available tools, and behavioral constraints

triples.subject: structured output
triples.object: agent response to match JSON schema

triples.subject: guardrails
triples.object: safety and format constraints on agent output

triples.subject: guardrails
triples.object: output validators, content filters, or secondary LLM checks
